name: Secure Software Update
description: Secure update mechanisms ensure only authentic, current code is installed: the vendor signs each release, the client downloads it and verifies the signature against the trusted key, rejects tampered packages, guards against downgrade attacks, and applies the update atomically with a post-install verification.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["New update released"]
    N2["Sign update (vendor)"]
    N3["Check for update"]
    N4["Download package"]
    N5["Verify signature"]
    N6{"Signature valid?"}
    N7["Reject + alert"]
    N8["Check version/rollback"]
    N9{"Downgrade attempt?"}
    N10["Apply atomically"]
    N11["Verify post-install"]
    N12["Updated + trusted"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N6 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 -->|yes| N7
    N9 -->|no| N10
    N10 --> N11
    N11 --> N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N12 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Survivable key compromise in software update systems (TUF)
sources.authors: Samuel, J.; Mathewson, N.; Cappos, J.; Dingledine, R.
sources.journal: ACM Conference on Computer and Communications Security (CCS)

sources.title: A look in the mirror: attacks on package managers
sources.authors: Cappos, J.; Samuel, J.; Baker, S.; Hartman, J. H.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
